nodes.name: Project Employee
nodes.desc: An employee assigned to a project. Resolves 'who is on this job'. Distinct from a generic employee — captures the project-specific assignment, role, and dates. Links to the standardized roles domain for project responsibility.

nodes.name: Labor Allocation
nodes.desc: Planned or actual allocation of labor hours to a cost code, WBS, or scope. Links employees → cost codes → budget. The operational unit for labor planning. Variance between planned and actual allocation is a cost overrun signal.

nodes.name: Daily Timecard
nodes.desc: Actual hours worked by an employee on a day, against a project and cost code. The source of truth for labor cost. Pairs with production quantities to compute productivity. Overtime hours flagged separately.

nodes.name: Time Off
nodes.desc: Approved leave or absence — affects available labor hours for planning. Tracks PTO, sick, holiday, jury duty. Reduces effective labor capacity for resource planning.

nodes.name: Equipment Project Log
nodes.desc: An equipment unit assigned to a project — start/end dates, ownership (owned vs rented), rate. Resolves 'what equipment is on site'. Drives equipment cost allocation and utilization tracking.

nodes.name: Equipment Timecard
nodes.desc: Hours an equipment unit operated on a project — drives equipment cost allocation. Pairs with equipment project log to compute utilization.

nodes.name: Equipment Telematic
nodes.desc: IoT data from equipment — load factor, idle time, fuel consumption, location, engine hours. Source for utilization and productivity analysis. Adoption is uneven — most projects don't have telematic-instrumented equipment.

nodes.name: Equipment Maintenance Record
nodes.desc: Maintenance history for an equipment unit — affects availability and lifecycle cost. Preventive vs reactive maintenance distinguishable. High maintenance cost signals end-of-life or misuse.

nodes.name: Production Quantity
nodes.desc: Actual quantity of work installed against a cost code or scope — the 'what got built' measurement. Pairs with timecard hours to compute productivity (output ÷ input). Foundational measurement for field execution.

nodes.name: Budget Change Production Quantity
nodes.desc: Production quantity tied to a budget change — tracks rework or scope adjustments at the field level. Distinguishes 'extra work installed' from 'original scope installed'.

nodes.name: Labor & Equipment Productivity
nodes.desc: Derived metric: work installed ÷ labor or equipment hours. The fundamental measure of field execution. Compared to estimated productivity (from assemblies) to validate estimating accuracy and identify field underperformance.

nodes.name: Material Record
nodes.desc: Material on site — quantity, delivery date, cost code, supplier. Closes the loop on material consumption vs. estimate. Pairs with submittals (specs) and POs (commitment) to track full material lifecycle.

nodes.name: Field Productivity Code
nodes.desc: Classification code applied to field productivity records — categorizes labor/equipment hours and installed quantities by type of work. Maps to Budget Code structure so productivity rolls up to budget for variance analysis. The bridge between operational productivity tracking and financial reporting.

nodes.name: Productivity Rate
nodes.desc: Output per labor hour, by trade and scope. Benchmark for estimating accuracy and field performance. Compared across crews, projects, and historical baselines. Core KPI for self-perform GCs.

nodes.name: Equipment Utilization %
nodes.desc: Hours equipment operated ÷ hours equipment on site. Signals over-fleeting (low utilization, idle cost) and bottlenecks (high utilization, capacity constraint).

nodes.name: Labor Burn Rate
nodes.desc: Actual labor cost ÷ planned labor cost over time. Leading indicator of cost overrun on labor-heavy scopes. Trended weekly to project EAC.

nodes.name: Overtime Rate
nodes.desc: OT hours ÷ total hours. High OT signals understaffing or schedule pressure. Sustained high OT correlates with safety incidents and quality issues — leading indicator for risk.

nodes.name: Construction Work Package (CWP)
nodes.desc: A discrete, self-contained scope of construction work. Aligns engineering deliverables and material procurement to the construction sequence. CII AWP framework. Typically 6-8 week duration of work.

nodes.name: Engineering Work Package (EWP)
nodes.desc: Engineering deliverables aligned to a CWP — drawings, specs, vendor data needed to construct. EWP completion drives CWP readiness.

nodes.name: Installation Work Package (IWP)
nodes.desc: Field-level work package — typically 1 week of work for a crew. CWP broken into IWPs for daily/weekly execution. Made-ready process drives IWP release.

nodes.name: Path of Construction (POC)
nodes.desc: Sequenced visualization of all CWPs from foundations through commissioning. Drives the AWP plan.

nodes.name: Workface Planning
nodes.desc: Pre-execution preparation that ensures every IWP has all constraints removed: drawings, materials, equipment, labor, prerequisites. The made-ready check before work releases to the field.
